message:
fix(install): guard BASH_SOURCE[0] with default-value expansion for curl|bash compatibility

When install.sh is invoked via curl|bash, BASH_SOURCE[0] is unset
(since the script is read from stdin, not sourced from a file). Under
set -u or strict bash modes, referencing BASH_SOURCE[0] without a
default causes an "unbound variable" error that silently kills the
installer.

Two fixes:
1. install_agent_skills(): ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-} prevents error when
   resolving the script directory for bundled skill discovery
2. Main guard: ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-} == "${0:-}" with fallback
   -z "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" covers both file-invoked and stdin-piped
   execution modes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3013,7 +3013,7 @@ install_agent_skills() {
 
   local script_dir=""
   local script_dir_candidate=""
-  if script_dir_candidate="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)"; then
+  if script_dir_candidate="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)"; then
     script_dir="$script_dir_candidate"
   fi
   local bundled_candidate=""
@@ -3376,6 +3376,6 @@ main() {
   print_summary
 }
 
-if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "$0" ]]; then
+if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" == "${0:-}" ]] || [[ -z "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" ]]; then
   main "$@"
 fi
